req.title: Delete patient (soft)
req.statement: A system-admin shall soft-delete a patient resource; deletion shall not remove history.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-004 mandates soft-deletion of patient resources by system-admin actors, preserving history while marking the resource as deleted. The requirement specifies three critical paths: (1) successful soft-delete by admin (positive), (2) rejection when non-admin attempts deletion (negative), and (3) edge cases around resource state and retrieval after deletion (boundary). Test design applies Equivalence Partitioning (admin vs. non-admin roles), Decision Table (authorization matrix), Negative Path (403 rejection), State Transition Coverage (deleted→history-accessible), and Boundary Value Analysis (soft vs. hard delete semantics).

tests.name: test_admin_soft_deletes_patient_successfully
expected: patient resource marked with meta.versionId incremented
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_admin_soft_deletes_patient_successfully(base_url):
    admin_token = '<admin_token>'
    patient_id = 'PT-001'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {admin_token}'}
    
    response = requests.delete(
        f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code in [200, 204], \
        f'Expected 200/204, got {response.status_code}: {response.text}'
    
    # Verify resource is marked deleted, not purged
    get_resp = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    deleted_patient = get_resp.json()
    assert 'meta' in deleted_patient
    assert deleted_patient.get('resourceType') == 'Patient'

tests.name: test_non_admin_delete_rejected_with_403
preconditions: non-admin user is authenticated (e.g., clinician role)
actions: verify response status code and error message
expected: error response includes OperationOutcome with issue.code='forbidden'
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_non_admin_delete_rejected_with_403(base_url):
    clinician_token = '<clinician_token>'
    patient_id = 'PT-002'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {clinician_token}'}
    
    response = requests.delete(
        f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 403, \
        f'Expected 403, got {response.status_code}'
    
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get('resourceType') == 'OperationOutcome'
    assert any(
        issue.get('code') == 'forbidden'
        for issue in body.get('issue', [])
    ), 'No forbidden issue found in OperationOutcome'

tests.name: test_deleted_patient_retrievable_via_history
preconditions: patient has at least 2 versions in history
expected: _history endpoint returns Bundle with all historical versions
deleted version entry has meta.versionId set
trace: R-HLT-004: soft-delete does not remove history
R-HLT-004: historical retrieval after deletion succeeds
R-HLT-004: state transition from active→deleted is auditable
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_deleted_patient_retrievable_via_history(base_url):
    admin_token = '<admin_token>'
    patient_id = 'PT-003'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {admin_token}'}
    
    # Soft-delete
    del_resp = requests.delete(
        f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert del_resp.status_code in [200, 204]
    
    # Retrieve history
    hist_resp = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = hist_resp.json()
    
    assert bundle.get('resourceType') == 'Bundle'
    entries = bundle.get('entry', [])
    assert len(entries) >= 2, \
        f'Expected ≥2 history entries, got {len(entries)}'
    
    # Verify deleted version has versionId
    latest = entries[-1].get('resource', {})
    assert 'meta' in latest
    assert 'versionId' in latest['meta']

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_delete_rejected_with_401
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_delete_rejected_with_401(base_url):
    patient_id = 'PT-004'
    
    response = requests.delete(
        f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}'
    )
    
    assert response.status_code == 401, \
        f'Expected 401, got {response.status_code}'
    assert len(response.text) > 0, 'No error body returned'

tests.name: test_soft_delete_idempotent_on_already_deleted
preconditions: patient resource already soft-deleted with id=PT-005
resource remains accessible but marked deleted
actions: verify response status and resource state
expected: resource state unchanged (no additional version created or 409 conflict)
trace: R-HLT-004: repeated deletes do not corrupt state
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_soft_delete_idempotent_on_already_deleted(base_url):
    admin_token = '<admin_token>'
    patient_id = 'PT-005'
    headers = {'Authorization': f'Bearer {admin_token}'}
    
    # First delete
    resp1 = requests.delete(
        f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp1.status_code in [200, 204]
    
    # Get versionId after first delete
    get_resp1 = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    version1 = get_resp1.json().get('meta', {}).get('versionId')
    
    # Second delete (idempotent)
    resp2 = requests.delete(
        f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp2.status_code in [200, 204, 409]
    
    # Verify no spurious version increment
    get_resp2 = requests.get(
        f'{base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}',
        headers=headers
    )
    version2 = get_resp2.json().get('meta', {}).get('versionId')
    assert version1 == version2 or resp2.status_code == 409
